methods.description: This method involves reconstructing the phase space of an irregular time series to analyze the dynamics of the system that produced the signal. It is used to unfold the dynamics of the system completely by embedding the time series in a higher-dimensional space.

steps.description: The irregular time series is linearly interpolated to a regular time step, 4 yr in the results shown here.
steps.searchable_summary: interpolate time series to regular 4-year intervals

steps.description: Data after the last DO is discarded for producing the phase space reconstruction.
steps.searchable_summary: discard data after last DO event

steps.description: After detrending, an optimal time lag has to be empirically chosen, guided by the first minimum of the average mutual information function (20 yr in our case).
steps.searchable_summary: detrend time series and choose optimal time lag using mutual information

steps.description: With copies of the regular time series lagged by multiples of the optimal time lag as coordinates, a phase space reconstruction is obtained.
steps.searchable_summary: perform phase space reconstruction using lagged time series

steps.description: The number of dimensions is chosen as the minimum one that brings the fraction of empirical global false neighbours to the background value.
steps.searchable_summary: determine embedding dimension using global false neighbours

steps.description: The Scientific Tools for Python (SciPy) implementation of the kd-tree algorithm is used for computing distances in the n-dimensional space.
steps.searchable_summary: compute distances using SciPy kd-tree algorithm

steps.description: The results are converted to a polar coordinate system and the anomaly from the average is considered.
steps.searchable_summary: convert results to polar coordinates and calculate anomaly

steps.description: The PDFs for the coordinates distributions are computed through a gaussian kernel estimation.
steps.searchable_summary: compute PDFs using gaussian kernel estimation

steps.description: The error of the PDFs is defined as the standard deviation of an ensemble of 50,000 synthetic datasets, obtained by bootstrapping.

methods.description: This method analyzes fluctuations in the ice core record to identify early warning signals (EWSs) of tipping points. It involves computing EWSs in a sliding window and analyzing the variance and trends in the time series.

steps.description: The EWSs of the time series are computed in a sliding window of 250 yr wide, and the results are plotted at the right end of the sliding window to avoid contamination from points after the DO onset.
The time series is first linearly interpolated to a step of 4 yr and approximately 60 points are used in the window.
The data is linearly detrended in the window before the computation, to compensate for potential nonstationarities in the time series.
In the computation of the DFA exponent, 10 boxes lengths were used, ranging from 16 to 52 yr.
steps.searchable_summary: compute DFA exponent with box lengths 16-52 years

steps.description: Different time steps for the interpolation and boxes lengths were tested. The results show small sensitivity to these choices, as long as the time step is kept below approximately 10 yr.
steps.searchable_summary: test sensitivity to interpolation and box lengths
